title: Plaid access tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Plaid access token detected in source code (UUID format). Plaid access tokens provide direct access to users' bank account data and financial information through the Plaid API. Exposing these tokens allows attackers to read transaction history, account balances, identity information, and income data. Access tokens are tied to specific Items (bank connections) and grant full read access to that connection. Store tokens securely in encrypted databases or secrets managers, never in source code.

Plaid access token detected in source code (alphanumeric format). Plaid access tokens are permanent credentials that grant access to users' financial accounts. These tokens enable reading transaction data, account balances, routing numbers, identity verification, income information, and asset data. Compromised access tokens allow attackers to access sensitive banking information for all accounts linked to that Item. Immediately rotate exposed tokens and store them securely using environment variables or secrets management systems.

Plaid access token detected in explicit assignment statement. This is a high-confidence detection of a Plaid access token being hardcoded in source code. Access tokens are the most sensitive Plaid credential type, granting permanent access to linked bank accounts and financial data. Exposing these tokens represents a critical security vulnerability. Remove the hardcoded token immediately, rotate it through the Plaid dashboard, and implement secure credential storage using environment variables, AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, or similar secrets management solutions.

Plaid access token detected in Authorization header. Bearer tokens in authorization headers provide direct API access to Plaid services. Exposing these tokens in source code allows attackers to authenticate as your application, access users' bank account data, retrieve transaction history, read account balances and routing numbers, and access identity and income verification data. Use secure credential storage and rotate tokens immediately if exposed. Never commit authorization headers with real tokens to version control.
